checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: Derived from quarterly AR and revenue figures in 10-K FY2014

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or InventoryGross concept reported in the provided data set; company reports SalesRevenueServicesNet and CostOfServices consistent with a services business
GrossProfit=276330954.86 (2014) / SalesRevenueServicesNet=510500608.32 (2014) = 54.1% margin, vs GrossProfit=249719456.44 (2013)/SalesRevenueServicesNet=453889907.19 (2013)=55.0% margin - stable

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly 2014: -641501.45 (Q1), -968258.75 (Q2), 2246456.39 (Q3), 6417417.14 (Q4) - showing significant swings from loss to profit within the year
NetIncomeLoss quarterly 2011: -1472810.45 (Q1), -3339651.75 (Q2), -4456873.38 (Q3) - increasing losses, not smoothed

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=588643.65 (2010-12-31), ratio to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=34345555.24 is 1.71%, dropping to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=245067.97 (2011-12-31) against AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=60812896.03, a ratio of only 0.40%, despite AR growing 77%

AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=28915617.86 (2012-12-31), 7.79% of SalesRevenueServicesNet=371234727.66 (2012), declining to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=19900960.76 (2014-12-31), only 3.90% of SalesRevenueServicesNet=510500608.32 (2014)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Form 4/A amendments filed 2012-03-09, 2012-11-13, and 2014-10-17, alongside an unusually high volume of Form 4 insider transaction filings throughout 2012-2015

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual point-in-time data available continuously from 2010-12-31 through 2014-12-31 across balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow concepts, filed across five 10-Ks and sixteen 10-Qs

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Premature or aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., recognizing revenue ahead of collectibility certainty in a reimbursement-sensitive service model) combined with insufficient bad-debt reserving, causing receivables and revenue to be overstated relative to underlying cash collections; evidenced by DSO expansion from ~79 days (2013) to ~94 days (2014) while revenue growth decelerated sharply (12.5% in 2014 vs 22.3% in 2013).

rationale_evidence.location: Derived quarterly ratio from 10-K FY2014 data

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-provisioning of the allowance for doubtful accounts relative to a rapidly growing receivables base, which reduces bad debt expense and overstates net income and net receivables carrying value.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=588643.65 (2010-12-31) equal to 1.71% of AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=34345555.24, falling to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=245067.97 (2011-12-31), only 0.40% of AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=60812896.03

Allowance ratio recovered only modestly to 1.03% by 2014 (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1412744.77 / AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=136824811.47), still below the 2010 baseline despite a ~4x growth in AR since 2010

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-accrual of operating expenses (e.g., bonuses, warranty, legal or other accrued liabilities) as the accrued liabilities balance failed to scale with revenue growth, potentially understating expenses and overstating operating income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=28915617.86 (2012-12-31), 7.79% of SalesRevenueServicesNet=371234727.66 (2012), falling to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=19900960.76 (2014-12-31), only 3.90% of SalesRevenueServicesNet=510500608.32 (2014)

top_signals: Receivables grew far faster than revenue in FY2014 (AR +31.6% vs revenue +12.5%), with DSO expanding from ~79 to ~94 days

Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of gross receivables fell sharply from 1.71% (2010) to as low as 0.40% (2011) despite AR nearly doubling, and remained below historical baseline through 2014
Accrued liabilities declined as a percentage of revenue from 7.79% (2012) to 3.90% (2014), suggesting potential under-accrual of expenses
Multiple filing amendments (10-K/A, 8-K/A, Form 4/A) present in the chronology, though no NT filings or late 10-K/10-Q were observed
